prose:
Frontier-orbital HOMO/LUMO gap — DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted) with PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: single-point on the user-supplied /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/furan/furan.xyz coordinates (no further relaxation; thus a DFT//input-xyz protocol). SCF converged in 9 cycles.

Headline result
• HOMO = −6.3597638 eV
• LUMO = −0.1024036 eV
→ HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.2573602 eV (Koopmans-type orbital gap, not an excitation energy)

No warnings were reported by the engine (`warnings` array was empty). Integrity check: status ok, trustworthy = true.

The calculation ran with the chemkit “frontier-orbitals” skill calling the PySCF backend; the JSON output is in /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpy1pqje36/live.json. The live engine log was written during execution to the standard chemkit frontier-orbitals `.out` file in the working directory (path provided at job start).
